data.narrative:
Newspace Research, a Series B defense & security company founded by Sameer Joshi, is evaluated against a comp universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. Total capital raised to date is $73.2M.

Two valuation methods are triangulated here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $73M raised proxy, implying $139M. Peer-median analysis draws on 7 same-sector, same-stage companies, identifying a median valuation of $1.0B against a range of $300M to $3.0B; named peers include Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B).

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $118M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $570M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in the peer set and tension between capital-raised-proxy methods and sector comps. Key uncertainties include whether Newspace's revenue, margins, and growth trajectory align with the median or outlier peers, and the extent to which defense & security fundamentals support valuations near the $1B mark at Series B stage. The median estimate of $570M anchors the range but should be stress-tested against specific operational metrics and market position relative to the peer sample.
